Results.Query: Write an apology letter to a friend whose birthday I missed
Results.ActualAnswer:
<thinking>
The user is asking me to write an apology letter. This is a creative/linguistic writing task — Domain A. No need to search Wikipedia.
</thinking>

Here's a heartfelt apology letter you can personalize:

---

**Subject: I'm So Sorry I Missed Your Birthday**

Dear [Friend's Name],

I am writing this with a heavy heart and a lot of regret. I missed your birthday, and there is simply no excuse good enough to justify it. You deserved to be celebrated, and I let you down — and I am truly, deeply sorry.

You are one of the most important people in my life, and the fact that I wasn't there to mark such a special day for you is something I genuinely feel awful about. Whether it was poor planning, a hectic schedule, or just a terrible oversight on my part, none of it changes the fact that you should have been my priority, and you weren't. That's on me.

I want you to know that your birthday — and more importantly, *you* — matter to me more than I clearly showed. You have always shown up for me, and I failed to do the same for you when it counted. I am not going to make excuses; I am just going to own it and say: I'm sorry.

I would love the chance to make it up to you. Whether that's taking you out for a belated celebration, a dinner of your choosing, or simply spending some quality time together — I want to make sure you feel appreciated and valued, even if it's a little late.

I hope you can find it in your heart to forgive me. Our friendship means the world to me, and I promise to do better.

With love and sincere apologies,
[Your Name]

---

> ✏️ **Tips to personalize it:**
> - **Add a specific memory** — mention something you love about your friend to make it more heartfelt.
> - **Mention a plan** — if you already have a makeup celebration in mind, include it!
> - **Match your tone** — if your friendship is more casual or humorous, feel free to lighten the tone slightly.

Results.Query: Who was the first president of the United States?
Results.ActualAnswer: **George Washington** was the first president of the United States, serving from **1789 to 1797** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/George_Washington). He was also a Founding Father and served as commander of the Continental Army before his presidency [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/George_Washington).

Results.Query: What is the population of Tokyo?
Results.ActualAnswer:
Tokyo's population figures are as follows:

- **City proper:** Over **14 million** residents (as of 2023) [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tokyo)
- **Tokyo Metropolis:** **14,195,730** (representing 11.5% of Japan's total population) [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Demographics_of_Tokyo)
- **Greater Tokyo Area** (Tokyo + parts of six neighboring prefectures): **41 million** residents, making it the most populous metropolitan area in the world (as of 2024) [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tokyo)

Results.Query: What year did World War II end?
Results.ActualAnswer:
World War II ended in **1945**. More specifically:

- The war in **Europe** ended in **May 1945**, following the fall of Nazi Germany [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/End_of_World_War_II_in_Europe).
- The war in **Asia** ended on **September 2, 1945**, with the formal surrender of Japan aboard the USS Missouri [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/End_of_World_War_II_in_Asia).

September 2, 1945 is generally recognized as the official end date of World War II globally.

Results.Query: Who invented the telephone?
Results.ActualAnswer:
**Alexander Graham Bell** is credited with inventing the telephone. In **1876**, Bell was the first to be granted a United States patent for a device that produced clearly intelligible replication of the human voice at a second device [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Telephone). He was a Scottish-born inventor, scientist, and engineer who also co-founded the American Telephone and Telegraph Company (AT&T) [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alexander_Graham_Bell).

It is worth noting that the invention has been historically contested. Italian inventor **Antonio Meucci** is also recognized by some as having developed an early voice communication device prior to Bell [3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antonio_Meucci), and the U.S. Congress passed a resolution in 2002 honoring his contributions to the invention of the telephone.

Results.Query: What is the speed of light?
Results.ActualAnswer:
The speed of light in vacuum, commonly denoted **c**, is a universal physical constant exactly equal to **299,792,458 m/s** (metres per second) [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light). This is approximately:

- **1 billion km/h** (1,000,000,000 kilometres per hour)
- **700 million mph** (miles per hour)

A few key facts about *c*:

- **It is exact by definition:** The metre itself is defined as the distance light travels in vacuum in 1/299,792,458 of a second [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light).
- **Universal speed limit:** It is the upper limit for the speed at which information, matter, or energy can travel through space [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light).
- **Observer-independent:** The speed of light is the same for all observers, regardless of their relative velocity or the motion of the light source [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light).
- **Slower in materials:** Light travels slower than *c* in transparent materials (e.g., ~200,000 km/s in glass, with a refractive index of ~1.5) [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light).
- **Famous relation:** Albert Einstein showed that *c* appears in the mass–energy equivalence **E = mc²** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light).

Results.Query: Who built the Space Needle?
Results.ActualAnswer:
The Space Needle was designed through a collaboration between **Edward E. Carlson** and architect **John Graham, Jr.**, and was constructed by the **Howard S. Wright Construction Company** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Space_Needle).

- The architecture is the result of a compromise between the designs of Edward E. Carlson and John Graham, Jr. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Space_Needle)
- It was built for the **1962 World's Fair** (Century 21 Exposition) in Seattle, Washington, and was located at the Seattle Center [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Space_Needle).
- The general contractor, **Howard S. Wright Companies**, was responsible for the physical construction [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Howard_S._Wright_Companies).

Results.Query: Tell me about Apple, the famous technology company
Results.ActualAnswer:
Here is a comprehensive overview of **Apple Inc.**:

---

### 🍎 Apple Inc. — Overview

**Apple Inc.** is an American multinational technology company headquartered in **Cupertino, California**, in Silicon Valley, known for consumer electronics, software, and online services [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).

---

### 🏛️ Founding & History
- Apple was **founded in 1976** as *Apple Computer Company* by **Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne**, and was incorporated the following year [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).
- It was originally created to market Wozniak's **Apple I** personal computer. Its successor, the **Apple II**, became one of the first successful mass-produced microcomputers [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).
- Apple introduced the **Lisa (1983)** and the iconic **Macintosh (1984)** — among the first computers to feature a **graphical user interface and a mouse** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).
- By **1985**, internal conflicts led to Jobs leaving to found **NeXT**, and the company struggled through the 1990s, nearly reaching **bankruptcy by 1997** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).
- Apple's turnaround came when it **acquired NeXT**, bringing **Jobs back**. Under his leadership, Apple launched the **iMac, iPod, iPhone, and iPad**, reviving the company's fortunes [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).
- The company was **renamed Apple Inc. in 2007** as its focus shifted from computers to broader consumer electronics [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).
- Jobs resigned in **2011** due to health reasons and passed away the same year, succeeded as CEO by **Tim Cook** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).

---

### 📱 Products & Services
Apple's lineup includes:
- **Hardware:** iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple Watch, AirPods
- **Operating Systems:** iOS, iPadOS, macOS
- **Services & Software:** Apple Pay, iCloud, Apple Music, Apple TV [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.)

---

### 💰 Market Position
- Apple has been largely the **world's largest company by market capitalization** since 2011 [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).
- It became the **first publicly traded US company valued at over $1 trillion** in 2018 [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).
- As of **October 2025**, Apple is valued at just over **$4 trillion** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).
- It is currently the **largest vendor of tablet computers**, the **largest vendor of mobile phones**, and the **largest manufacturing company by revenue** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).

---

### ⚠️ Criticism
Apple has faced criticism over **contractors' labor conditions, environmental practices, anti-competitive tactics, and materials sourcing**, among other corporate ethics issues [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).

---

Despite controversies, Apple enjoys a **massive global following and high customer loyalty**, and has consistently ranked as one of the **world's most valuable brands** since the late 2000s [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).

Results.Query: Did Einstein win a Nobel Prize for the theory of relativity?
Results.ActualAnswer:
No, Einstein did **not** win the Nobel Prize for the theory of relativity. He received the **1921 Nobel Prize in Physics** for **"his services to theoretical physics, and especially for his discovery of the law of the photoelectric effect"** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Albert_Einstein).

This is a common misconception. While Einstein is best known for developing the theory of relativity [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Albert_Einstein), the Nobel Committee awarded him the prize specifically for his explanation of the photoelectric effect — a foundational contribution to quantum theory.

Results.Query: Did Napoleon win the Battle of Waterloo?
Results.ActualAnswer:
No, Napoleon did **not** win the Battle of Waterloo — he was decisively defeated.

The Battle of Waterloo was fought on **18 June 1815**, and the **French Imperial Army under Napoleon I was defeated by two armies of the Seventh Coalition**: a British-led force under the Duke of Wellington, and a Prussian army under Field Marshal Blücher [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Waterloo).

Throughout the afternoon, Wellington withstood repeated French attacks, and was eventually aided by approximately **50,000 Prussians** who attacked the French flank. When Napoleon made a final assault with his elite Imperial Guard, it was repulsed, and the French army was routed [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Waterloo).

The consequences were catastrophic for Napoleon: he **abdicated four days later**, coalition forces entered Paris on 7 July, and the defeat marked the **end of the First French Empire** and Napoleon's definitive exile [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Waterloo). The battle became so synonymous with total defeat that the phrase *"meeting one's Waterloo"* entered popular culture as an expression for a catastrophic reversal [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Waterloo).

Results.Query: Did the Titanic sink on its first voyage?
Results.ActualAnswer: Yes. The RMS Titanic sank on its **first (maiden) voyage**. It was four days into her maiden voyage from Southampton when it struck an iceberg and sank in the North Atlantic Ocean in the early hours of **15 April 1912** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Titanic) [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sinking_of_the_Titanic). The ship never completed a single crossing — it famously went to the bottom of the ocean before reaching its destination of New York City.

The Apple AirPower was **never officially priced**, as it was never released. AirPower is an unreleased wireless charging mat developed by Apple Inc., announced on September 12, 2017. It was designed to charge up to three devices simultaneously, supporting two Qi devices (such as an iPhone and AirPods) and an Apple Watch. Though formerly scheduled for release in early 2018, Apple ultimately announced on **March 29, 2019** that it had **canceled** the product entirely [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AirPower_(Apple)).

Since the AirPower never reached market, Apple never officially confirmed a retail price for it.
